violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety (with RIDOH Center for Food Protection) at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. Application left with PIC at time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

Raw animal foods (such as eggs) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as tomatoes) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was broken down and moved to ware-washing area to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

The hand sink next to the prep table and 3-bay sink is lacking a proper splash guard to protect from possible water splashing during prep time. A splash guard (smooth, durable, easily cleanable) must be installed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine and quaternary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink in the kitchen and bar. New Violation. Correct By: 03/30/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine has an accumulation of biofilm. The ice machine must be cleaned as often as necessary to prevent the possible contamination of ice. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026
The ceiling tiles in the kitchen area is in poor repair (broken/peeling tiles). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 03/16/2026

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by boroughs and live rodent observed at the time of inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. No activity was observed inside of the establishment. Establishment to increase pest control service. Establishment to follow pest control recommendations. Reports to be reviewed during next inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 12/18/2023

At the time of inspection, an open container of Chic Peas stored in the outdoor storage container was observed to have rodent droppings within the Chic Peas. Chic Peas were disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2023

At the time of inspection, a dead rodent was observed in the outside area behind the dumpster units. Dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests shall be removed from control devices and the PREMISES at a frequency that prevents their accumulation, decomposition, or the attraction of pests. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2023

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings observed in the outside storage container. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2023

The drain plug for the outside dumpster is missing. Drains in receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must have drain plugs in place. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/28/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the Outdoor Storage Bin and the outdoor area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 06/30/2023
A dead rodent was observed behind the dumpster. Dead rodents shall be removed as frequently as possible. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2023

The grease trap outside is located on gravel. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2023

The drain plug for the outside dumpster is missing. Drains in receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must have drain plugs in place. New Violation. Correct By: 06/20/2023

Marinara, which was previously cooked and cooled, was only reheated to 118 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. Proper reheating methods reviewed with pic. Per PIC, sauce was recently placed in hot hold unit. Marinara was reheated. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/10/2023

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at ALL times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. More employee/s to register to become certified in food safety. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer, ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of various debris: 1. The can opener and slicer were observed with an accumulation of debris. Proper sanitizing procedures reviewed with PIC. Deli slicing cleaning handout provided for PIC. 2. The ice machine was observed with an accumulation of bio-film. Ice machine was turned off and taken out of service. PIC to call for machine to be serviced. All equipment must be sanitized prior to use. Ice was disposed of.The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2023

Canned cheese was only heated to 42 degrees Fahrenheit before being placed into hot holding. Ready to eat foods taken from a commercially processed can or intact package from a food processing plant must be heated to at least 135 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding. Cheese was relocated to stove top. Proper reheating methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2023

Multiple foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation or incorrect date. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2023

The door to the outside is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Bowls were tossed out. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/20/2023
The sliding door in the kitchen was not in good repair (falling off of the track). 3-bay sink is lacking stoppers. Stoppers must be purchased. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023
The receptacle in the women's toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2023
